summary: Matched src/melee/it/items/itnesspkfirepillar.c for Ness PK Fire pillar/flame item logic. The PR replaced several address-style symbols with item-specific itNesspkfirepillar names, added the item implementation, introduced a small special-attributes struct, wired the item logic table to the newly named handlers, adjusted local symbol metadata for literals, and moved the object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
what_changed: Implemented itnesspkfirepillar.c, expanding it from a small stub for it_802AA7C4 into a matched item source file with spawn, initialization, animation, physics, collision, damage-received, and event callback logic.

Added itNesspkfirepillar_802AA494 to initialize a SpawnItem for It_Kind_Ness_PKFire_Flame, create it with Item_80268B18, link it to debug/item parent handling via db_80225DD8, and transition it through itNesspkfirepillar_802AA55C.
Added itNesspkfirepillar_802AA55C to fetch itNessPKFirepillarAttributes from article special attributes, set motion 0 with Item_80268E5C, and initialize lifetime with it_80275158.
Added animation scale logic that interpolates between attrs->scale and 1.0F based on item->xD44_lifeTimer and attrs->x0, then multiplies by item attribute scale.

Documented and intentionally reproduced apparent assignment-in-condition behavior in Phys and Coll handlers, noting the bug that the item is unintentionally set to GA_Air, with references to similar itNokonoko handlers.
Added damage-received logic that adjusts item->xD44_lifeTimer using item->xC9C and attrs->x4, with a lower bound supplied through get_min_life().

Renamed symbols from generic names such as it_802AA494, it_802AA55C, it_2725_Logic24_DmgReceived, and it_802AA7C4 to item-specific names including itNesspkfirepillar_Logic24_DmgReceived and itNesspkfirepillar_Logic24_EvtUnk.
Updated it_2725.c item logic table entry for Ness PK fire pillar to call the renamed damage-received and event-unknown handlers.
Added itNessPKFirepillarAttributes to itCommonItems.h with fields x0, x4, and scale, while also adding explicit offset comments to itMDisableAttributes.
Updated itnesspkfirepillar.h declarations to expose the newly matched functions and changed the item state table declaration to extern ItemStateTable it_803F6B60[1].
Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itnesspkfirepillar.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt sdata range for itnesspkfirepillar.c from ending at 0x804D53E8 to 0x804D53E5.
Converted several sdata and sdata2 symbols used by this file from global it_804D... names to local labels such as @182, @183, @144, @154, and @199.
smart_moves: Used item-specific symbol names for logic callbacks, making the item logic table and symbols.txt more searchable and less dependent on raw addresses.

Preserved a compiler/linker matching trick with get_min_life returning 1.0F; the inline comment records that the helper may be dead-stripped by the linker while still influencing constant ordering or codegen enough to match.
Factored SpawnItem setup into an inline helper, which keeps the matched spawn function readable while allowing call-site codegen control.
Reproduced suspicious original-code behavior, including assignment inside conditionals for ground_or_air, and explicitly marked it as a bug rather than silently 'fixing' it.
Pulled special item attributes into a named struct, improving future work around article-data access while leaving unknown fields as x0 and x4 where semantics were not proven.
Marked file status as Matching only after updating source, symbols, splits, and logic table references together, reducing risk of build/config drift.
decomp_lessons: For matching work, do not correct apparent retail bugs if the assignment or odd behavior is required by the original code; document them with //! @bug comments instead.
A tiny helper returning a float constant can be useful as a matching tactic when constant ordering or linker dead stripping affects the final object.
When a file becomes matched, update configure.py, symbols.txt, and any split section bounds in the same PR so objdiff/build configuration reflects the new source.
Use local @-numbered labels for file-local literals in symbols.txt when matching reveals that previous global it_804D... symbols are not externally meaningful.
Prefer naming callbacks by item and logic slot, such as itNesspkfirepillar_Logic24_DmgReceived, when the item identity and table role are known.
If special-attributes field semantics are not clear, a named attributes struct with offset-based placeholder fields is better than inventing behavior-specific names.
naming_conventions: Item file functions use the prefix itNesspkfirepillar_ followed by either an address suffix or a logic/motion role.
Motion callbacks follow the pattern itNesspkfirepillar_UnkMotion0_Anim, itNesspkfirepillar_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itNesspkfirepillar_UnkMotion0_Coll.
Logic table callbacks use role names such as itNesspkfirepillar_Logic24_DmgReceived and itNesspkfirepillar_Logic24_EvtUnk.
Unknown but item-specific article attributes were named itNessPKFirepillarAttributes, with unresolved fields retained as x0 and x4 plus a semantically clearer scale field.
File-local literals in config/GALE01/symbols.txt were represented as local labels like @182 and @144 rather than global address-derived names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved itnesspkfirepillar.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...) after matching the file.
Used PAD_STACK(0x10) in the spawn function, indicating stack-shape control was needed for the match.
Added get_min_life() returning 1.0F, with comments indicating it was used to handle a 1.0F versus 0.0F constant-ordering/codegen issue and expected to be linker dead-stripped.
Used inline helper functions for SpawnItem initialization and animation scale/lifetime handling, likely to preserve the original instruction structure while keeping the source maintainable.
Kept assignment expressions in conditionals for item->ground_or_air = GA_Air to match retail behavior despite being logically bug-like.
Adjusted sdata and sdata2 symbol scopes to local labels and updated the sdata split end address, reflecting refined section/literal ownership after matching.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files using helper functions that are expected to be dead-stripped for float constant matching.
Search for other assignment-in-condition item bugs documented with //! @bug and references to itNokonoko handlers.
Review nearby Ness item files such as itnesspkfire.c, itnesspkflush.c, itnesspkthunderball.c, and itnesspkthundertrail.c for comparable naming and attributes struct patterns.
Check whether itNessPKFirepillarAttributes fields x0 and x4 are later renamed with stronger semantic evidence.
